trip.name: Chill Food and History in Hong Kong

days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring the local culture

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: Your flight arrives at Hong Kong International Airport. After clearing immigration, make your way to the airport express train that takes you to the city in around 24 minutes.

timeline.title: Take Airport Express to Hong Kong Station
timeline.detail: Enjoy a comfortable train ride aboard the Airport Express, which provides stunning views of the city as you travel towards Hong Kong Central.

timeline.title: Check in to Kimberly Hotel
timeline.detail: Located a short walk from Tsim Sha Tsui station, the Kimberly Hotel offers comfortable accommodation and easy access to nearby attractions. Freshen up before heading out.
timeline.mapQuery: Kimberly Hotel, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Enjoy a traditional Hong Kong dish of wonton noodles at Mak's Noodle, known for its flavorful broth and handmade noodles. A must-try in the city, perfect for a quick meal.

timeline.title: Visit the Hong Kong Museum of History
timeline.detail: Explore the rich history of Hong Kong through interactive exhibits and artifacts. This museum provides great insights into the city's journey from fishing village to global metropolis.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong Museum of History, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Victoria Harbour Ferry Ride
timeline.detail: Experience the iconic ferry ride across Victoria Harbour. Enjoy breathtaking views of the skyline during sunset, an unforgettable photo opportunity.

timeline.title: Dinner at Tsim Chai Kee
timeline.detail: Savor delicious beef brisket noodles at Tsim Chai Kee, a local favorite. Be sure to try their big bowl meal, which offers a generous portion at a reasonable price.

localTips: Get an Octopus card at the airport for seamless travel on public transport.
Be sure to explore the nearby Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade for stunning views.
Try local street food for hidden gems throughout the city.

days.title: Discovering Kowloon
days.subtitle: Culinary delights and local markets

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a traditional Hong Kong-style breakfast at Australia Dairy Company, famous for its toast and milk tea. Don't miss the fast service - it's a local favorite!

timeline.title: Visit Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Explore the colorful Wong Tai Sin Temple, famous for its fortune telling and beautiful architecture. It's a great place to soak in the local spirituality and culture.
timeline.mapQuery: Wong Tai Sin Temple, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Shopping at Mong Kok
timeline.detail: Head to Mong Kok, one of the busiest districts in the world. Explore its vibrant street markets, including the Ladies Market for unique souvenirs and fashion items.

timeline.title: Lunch at Sister Wah
timeline.detail: Enjoy a delicious bowl of beef brisket noodles at Sister Wah, a renowned spot in the area. Known for its tender meat and flavorful broth, this dish is a must-try.

timeline.title: Explore the Yau Ma Tei Fruit Market
timeline.detail: Wander through the Yau Ma Tei Fruit Market, where you can find various fresh fruits and enjoy the vibrant atmosphere. Great for picking up local produce or just snapping pictures.
timeline.mapQuery: Yau Ma Tei Fruit Market, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Joy Hing Roasted Meat
timeline.detail: Indulge in Hong Kong's famous roasted meats at Joy Hing. Try the char siu (BBQ pork) and siu yuk (crispy pork belly) for an authentic experience.
timeline.mapQuery: Joy Hing Roasted Meat, Hong Kong

localTips: Try to arrive early at breakfast spots to avoid long lines.
Haggle at street markets; it's part of the fun.
Public transport is efficient, use your Octopus card to pay seamlessly.

days.title: Cultural Insights and Farewell
days.subtitle: Final local adventures

timeline.title: Breakfast at DimDimSum Dim Sum Specialty Store
timeline.detail: Enjoy a dim sum breakfast at DimDimSum, acclaimed for its variety of tasty small dishes. Perfect for indulging in traditional Hong Kong cuisine.

timeline.title: Visit Hong Kong Cultural Centre
timeline.detail: Walk over to the Hong Kong Cultural Centre, which hosts various performing arts exhibitions. Check out what's on during your visit and enjoy the architecture.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong Cultural Centre, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Dine at Tim Ho Wan, one of the world's most affordable Michelin-starred restaurants, known for its delicious baked buns and dumplings. Be prepared for a wait as it's very popular!

timeline.title: Visit Hong Kong Park
timeline.detail: Relax and unwind at Hong Kong Park, featuring beautiful gardens, ponds, and waterfalls. A peaceful escape from the bustling city streets.

timeline.title: MTR Ride to Central
timeline.detail: Take a ride on the MTR to Central, enjoy the efficiency and cleanliness of Hong Kong's public transport system. Use this opportunity to admire the city as you travel.

timeline.title: Dinner at Yung Kee Restaurant
timeline.detail: Finish your trip with a meal at the famous Yung Kee, known for its roast goose and traditional Cantonese dishes, a true culinary highlight.

localTips: Check for museum or transport pass deals for your last day.
Keep some cash on hand for local vendors and small eateries.
Take your time to savor final meals, enjoy the flavors of Hong Kong.

hotels.name: Kimberly Hotel
hotels.note: Located a short walk from Tsim Sha Tsui station.
hotels.address: 28 Kimberley Rd, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

budget.note: Round trip from airport to city.
For entry to museums and attractions.
